question: Will CPI rise more than 0.9% in March 2026?
reasoning: The March 2026 CPI report has been released and shows a 0.3% monthly increase, significantly below the 0.9% threshold required for YES resolution. Multiple reliable sources including BLS data and financial news outlets confirm the 0.3% figure. Some earlier forecasts predicted higher increases due to energy price spikes from Iran conflict, but the actual data shows inflation remained more contained.
key_factors: Official BLS data shows March CPI increased 0.3% month-over-month
0.3% increase is well below the 0.9% threshold for YES resolution
Market closed April 10, 2026 - the same day CPI data was released
Earlier forecasts of 0.8-0.9% increases due to energy spike did not materialize
risks: Data revision possibility though CPI revisions are typically minor
Potential misinterpretation of which CPI measure is being used
Market may not have fully processed the released data given timing
